//: Rosetta binding manifest for the Geogram library.
//paths: All relative paths resolve from THIS file's directory (the project root).
//design: GEO::Mesh is NOT bound directly: its data API lives in public member objects (mesh.vertices, mesh.facets, ...) that hold a Mesh& back-reference (non copy-assignable, so the generated def_readwrite would not compile) and expose raw pointers / GEO::vector containers no backend can marshal. Instead src/Mesh.{h,cpp} wraps GEO::Mesh behind a pImpl with flat std::vector accessors, and src/algorithms.{h,cpp} exposes the requested geogram algorithms (reconstruction, remeshing, parameterization/texturing, booleans, CSG) as one-line delegating free functions.
//sources: The geogram sources are compiled straight into every binding target (no prebuilt libgeogram: the wasm target needs the sources anyway, and a native lib built by another toolchain would risk ABI mismatch). Globs are non-recursive, one per source dir. Optional parts guarded by GEOGRAM_WITH_TETGEN / _TRIANGLE / _LUA / _PDEL compile away. The vendored zlib/rply/libMeshb/OpenNL are C sources (the generated projects enable_language(C)).
//defines: GEOGRAM_USE_BUILTIN_DEPS: mesh_io/geofile include the vendored libMeshb/rply/zlib instead of system libs. GEOGRAM_WITH_HLBFGS: compiles the HLBFGS optimizer in (required by the Newton iterations of CVT remeshing).
//version-h: src/geogram/version.h stands in for the header geogram's own CMake would generate; src/ is listed before extern/geogram/src/lib so it wins the include lookup.
//link-options: Under Node, geogram's GEO::initialize() mounts the host filesystem with NODEFS (EM_ASM in basic/common.cpp), so the wasm link needs emscripten's nodefs.js library — exactly what geogram's own cmake/platforms/Emscripten-clang.cmake adds.

classes.name: georo::Mesh
classes.doc: Triangulated surface mesh / point cloud (wraps GEO::Mesh). Geometry goes in and out as flat arrays: vertices [x0,y0,z0,...], triangles [v0,v1,v2,...]. load()/save() understand .obj, .off, .ply, .stl, .mesh/.meshb and .geogram.

functions.name: georo::initialize
functions.doc: Initialize geogram (done lazily by every function; call with verbose=true to enable geogram's logger).

functions.name: georo::co3ne_smooth
functions.doc: Smooth a point cloud by projection on the best approximating plane of the k nearest neighbors (Co3Ne).

functions.name: georo::co3ne_smooth_and_reconstruct
functions.doc: Smooth a point cloud and reconstruct a triangulated surface (Co3Ne). radius is the maximum distance used to connect neighbors with triangles.

functions.name: georo::remesh_smooth
functions.doc: Isotropic remeshing by Centroidal Voronoi Tesselation (nb_points samples, Lloyd + Newton optimization).

functions.name: georo::mesh_repair
functions.doc: Glue colocated vertices, remove degenerate/duplicate facets, connect and reorient facets.

functions.name: georo::fill_holes
functions.doc: Fill holes smaller than max_area with fewer than max_edges border edges.

functions.name: georo::tessellate_facets
functions.doc: Subdivide facets with more than max_nb_vertices vertices (3 triangulates the mesh).

functions.name: georo::make_atlas
functions.doc: Segment into charts, flatten (projection|lscm|spectral|abf) and pack (none|tetris|xatlas) in texture space; read the UVs back with Mesh.tex_coords().

functions.name: georo::get_charts
functions.doc: Number of charts of a parameterized mesh.

functions.name: georo::mesh_union
functions.doc: Boolean union of two closed surface meshes.

functions.name: georo::mesh_intersection
functions.doc: Boolean intersection of two closed surface meshes.

functions.name: georo::mesh_difference
functions.doc: Boolean difference of two closed surface meshes.

functions.name: georo::mesh_remove_intersections
functions.doc: Resolve the self-intersections of a surface mesh.

functions.name: georo::mesh_facets_have_intersection
functions.doc: Test whether two facets of a mesh intersect.

functions.name: georo::csg_evaluate_string
functions.doc: Evaluate an OpenSCAD-style .csg program from a string (sphere, cube, cylinder, union, difference, intersection, transforms, ...).
